verdict: The Burkina Faso government's August 2025 demand for an additional 35% of Kiaka SA is unresolved and constitutes a binary ownership risk over WAF's primary growth asset; until this resolves, the otherwise exceptional balance sheet (US$210m net cash at December 2025) and production growth trajectory (480,000 oz/year average target 2025-2034) cannot be valued with confidence.

hypothesis.bull: If Kiaka SA ownership resolves with WAF retaining 85%, group production targets 480,000 oz/year average (2025-2034) peaking at 569,000 oz in 2029; at FY2025 AISC of US$1,488/oz against US$3,525/oz average realised price, margins support rapid further cash accumulation and a material capital return programme -- WAF planned to outline its capital management strategy in Q1 2026.
hypothesis.bear: If the government acquires an additional 35% of Kiaka SA, WAF's attributable interest in Kiaka falls from 85% to approximately 50%; at Kiaka's 248,000 oz/year target, that reduction costs WAF approximately 87,000 oz/year of economic output; combined with Burkina Faso's sliding-scale royalty at 10% for gold above US$4,000/oz, a 1% community development levy, and mandatory 15% priority dividends on subsidiary profits, sovereign extraction could consume 25-30% of group revenue at current gold prices.

evidence.label: Kiaka SA ownership -- unresolved sovereign demand
evidence.finding: Fact: In August 2025, the Burkina Faso government formally requested an additional 35% of Kiaka SA for paid consideration; WAF was suspended from ASX from 1 September 2025 and reinstated 25 November 2025; discussions remain active as at the December 2025 quarterly. Inference: if granted at full scope, WAF's economic interest in Kiaka SA would fall from 85% to approximately 50% of the subsidiary that holds the primary growth asset.
evidence.source: WAF December 2025 Quarterly; WAF September 2025 Quarterly

evidence.label: 2024 Mining Code equity transfer -- complete
evidence.finding: Fact: State equity in SOMISA (Sanbrado), Kiaka SA, and Toega SA increased from 10% to 15% in July-August 2025 for nil proceeds, reducing WAF's interest from 90% to 85%; A$33.4m reduction in retained profits recorded in H1 2025 consolidated statement.

evidence.label: Royalty structure -- sliding scale, price-linked
evidence.finding: Fact: Burkina Faso imposes a gross production royalty at 7% for gold US$2,000-3,000/oz, rising by 1% per US$500/oz increment above US$3,000/oz (reaching 12% for gold above US$5,500/oz); plus a 1% community development levy payable at all levels; effective April 2025, the base royalty rate increased by 1%; at WAF's Q4 2025 average realised price of US$4,058/oz, the applicable royalty rate was 10%.
evidence.source: WAF December 2025 Quarterly; WAF H1 2025 Financial Report

evidence.label: Priority dividends -- non-discretionary confirmed
evidence.finding: Fact: The State's 15% equity in each subsidiary entitles it to an annual non-discretionary priority dividend equal to 15% of subsidiary profits under OHADA accounting principles; SOMISA paid A$99m to the State in Q3 2025 (15% of retained earnings to December 2024); WAF provisioned A$26.4m for the SOMISA H1 2025 priority dividend; Kiaka SA and Toega SA recorded no OHADA profit in H1 2025 so no provision was required for those entities.
evidence.source: WAF H1 2025 Financial Report; WAF September 2025 Quarterly

evidence.label: Balance sheet -- net cash by December 2025
evidence.finding: Fact: Net debt of A$132.8m at 30 June 2025 transitioned to notional net cash of US$33m (approximately A$51m) by 30 September 2025 and US$210m (approximately A$323m at 0.65 AUD/USD) by 31 December 2025; gross cash A$584m with A$177m unsold gold bullion at year-end; borrowings were secured facilities of US$265m combined limit from Sprott Resource Lending Corp and Coris Bank International, fully drawn at 30 June 2025; A$28m principal repaid in Q4 2025.
evidence.source: WAF December 2025 Quarterly; WAF September 2025 Quarterly; WAF H1 2025 Financial Report

evidence.label: FY2025 production and cost -- guidance met fifth consecutive year
evidence.finding: Fact: Group produced 300,383 oz (guidance 290,000-360,000 oz); group AISC US$1,488/oz (A$2,308/oz at 0.6448 average FX); average realised price US$3,525/oz (A$5,466/oz); Q4 operating cash flow A$389m after A$48m income tax; Q4 group AISC US$1,561/oz driven by sliding-scale royalty applied to US$4,058/oz average realised price.

evidence.label: Kiaka ramp-up cost trajectory
evidence.finding: Fact: Kiaka site sustaining cost fell from US$1,921/oz (Q3 2025, August-September only) to US$1,649/oz (Q4 2025, first full quarter); grid power contributed in December 2025, enabling higher throughput and treatment of harder, higher-grade ore; Kiaka 10-year target is 248,000 oz/year from 2026. Inference: Q4 is not steady-state; further cost improvement expected as full grid power replaces backup diesel generators and ore blend improves.
evidence.source: WAF December 2025 Quarterly; WAF September 2025 Quarterly

evidence.label: 10-year production target and mineral inventory
evidence.finding: Fact: Ore Reserves 6.5 Moz gold and Mineral Resources 12.2 Moz gold (both updated August 2025); 10-year production target averages 480,000 oz/year from 2025 to 2034, peaking at 569,000 oz in 2029; Sanbrado mine plan extended to 2035; Kiaka 248,000 oz/year target from 2026; 2026 annual guidance not yet released (planned Q1 2026). Reserve grade in g/t not stated in corpus.
evidence.source: WAF H1 2025 Financial Report; WAF September 2025 Quarterly

monitoring_trigger: Resolution of Kiaka SA ownership negotiations: any ASX announcement specifying the final equity structure of Kiaka SA before 30 June 2026; re-assess immediately on announcement as this is the binary determinant of the production growth thesis. Secondary trigger: WAF 2026 annual guidance release (expected Q1 2026) -- will confirm Kiaka steady-state cost and production on full grid power.
information_gaps: Kiaka SA ownership resolution terms: the quantum of any paid consideration, the final equity structure, and any conditions attached to the Burkina Faso government's acquisition are not disclosed in the corpus; this is the single most material unknown for WAF valuation.

Reserve grade (g/t): the 6.5 Moz Ore Reserve and 12.2 Moz Mineral Resource figures are confirmed in the corpus but g/t grades for Sanbrado and Kiaka are not stated; grade is required to model life-of-mine AISC trajectory.

Residual debt balance at December 2025: secured facilities were US$265m fully drawn at 30 June 2025; A$28m in Q4 principal repayments are disclosed but the residual balance is not stated; the US$210m notional net cash figure at year-end cannot be fully reconciled from corpus data.

Formal mine life statement: the 10-year production target to 2034/2035 is not a JORC-compliant mine life; reserve depletion rates and stripping ratios needed to verify the target are not in the corpus.

Gold price sensitivity: no formal scenario analysis (revenue or margin at US$2,200/oz, US$2,800/oz spot, US$3,500/oz) is present in the corpus; modelling the royalty and margin impact at lower gold prices requires assumptions not supported by the corpus.
